 `./openspec/changes/add-bun-global-packages/proposal.md`

```text
     1→# Change: Add Bun Global Package Installation
     2→
     3→## Why
     4→Bun is now a core Homebrew package, but there's no mechanism to install global bun packages (e.g., `ralph-tui`) from `packages.yaml`. The `bun` key already exists in the data file under the `ai` tag but nothing consumes it — mirroring a gap that UV tools once had before their installation script was added.
     5→
     6→## What Changes
     7→- Add a `run_onchange_before` installation script at position 26 that installs bun global packages from `packages.yaml`
     8→- Rename `run_onchange_before_darwin-26-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl` to position 28 so the machine-specific Brewfile script remains last in the package management group (20-29)
     9→- Update the package-management spec to document the `bun` key and its installation behavior
    10→- Update the tag reference table and installation sequence to include bun
    11→- Update the central package definition requirement to include `bun` as a recognized package type
    12→
    13→## Impact
    14→- Affected specs: `package-management`
    15→- Affected code:
    16→  - `home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-26-install-bun-packages.sh.tmpl` (new)
    17→  - `home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-26-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl` → renamed to `run_onchange_before_darwin-28-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl`
    18→  - `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (already has `bun` key — no changes needed)
    19→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 18:57:37Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-26-install-bun-packages.sh.tmpl`

```text
     1→{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
     2→#!/bin/bash
     3→
     4→source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"
     5→
     6→if ! require_tools bun; then
     7→    exit 1
     8→fi
     9→
    10→print_message "info" "Installing global packages using bun..."
    11→
    12→{{- $categories := prepend .tag_choices "core" }}
    13→{{- range $category := $categories }}
    14→  {{- $categoryData := index $.packages.darwin $category }}
    15→  {{- if or (eq $category "core") (has $category $.tags) }}
    16→    {{- if and (hasKey $categoryData "bun") $categoryData.bun }}
    17→# Install {{ $category }} bun packages
    18→{{ range $categoryData.bun -}}
    19→bun install -g {{ . }}
    20→{{ end -}}
    21→    {{- end }}
    22→  {{- end }}
    23→{{- end }}
    24→
    25→{{ end -}}
    26→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 18:57:38Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./openspec/changes/add-bun-global-packages/specs/package-management/spec.md`

```text
     1→## MODIFIED Requirements
     2→
     3→### Requirement: Central Package Definition
     4→All packages SHALL be defined in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` organized by platform and tag.
     5→
     6→#### Scenario: Platform-based package organization
     7→- **WHEN** packages are defined for the darwin platform
     8→- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin` with nested tag categories
     9→
    10→#### Scenario: Tag-based package categories
    11→- **WHEN** packages are categorized by tags (core, dev, ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
    12→- **THEN** each tag SHALL contain separate lists for `taps`, `brews`, `casks`, `mas`, `sdkman`, `uv`, and `bun` packages
    13→
    14→## ADDED Requirements
    15→
    16→### Requirement: Bun Global Package Definition
    17→Bun global packages SHALL be defined within tag categories in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `bun` key.
    18→
    19→#### Scenario: Package organization structure
    20→- **WHEN** bun packages are defined in `packages.yaml`
    21→- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.<tag>.bun` with lists of package names
    22→
    23→#### Scenario: Package specification format
    24→- **WHEN** a bun package is defined
    25→- **THEN** it SHALL use a simple package name format: `"package-name"`
    26→
    27→### Requirement: Tag-Based Bun Package Selection
    28→Bun global package installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags.
    29→
    30→#### Scenario: Tag-conditional packages
    31→- **WHEN** a user selects the `ai` tag
    32→- **THEN** packages in the `ai` tag's `bun` list SHALL be installed
    33→- **AND** packages in unselected tag categories SHALL be skipped
    34→
    35→#### Scenario: Multiple package categories
    36→- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai`
    37→- **THEN** bun packages from all three tag categories SHALL be installed via `bun install -g`
    38→
    39→### Requirement: Bun Global Package Installation Script
    40→Bun global packages SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 26.
    41→
    42→#### Scenario: Package installation execution
    43→- **WHEN** the bun package installation script runs
    44→- **THEN** it SHALL execute `bun install -g <package>` for each selected package
    45→- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
    46→
    47→#### Scenario: Package installation idempotency
    48→- **WHEN** a package is already installed globally
    49→- **THEN** `bun install -g` SHALL skip reinstallation or upgrade the package
    50→- **AND** SHALL continue with remaining packages
    51→
    52→#### Scenario: Bun availability
    53→- **WHEN** the bun package installation script runs
    54→- **THEN** bun SHALL already be available via Homebrew (installed at position 23 as a core brew)
    55→- **AND** the script SHALL validate bun is present using `require_tools`
    56→
    57→### Requirement: Bun Package Categories
    58→Bun packages SHALL be organized within the same tag categories as other package types in `packages.yaml`.
    59→
    60→#### Scenario: AI package category
    61→- **WHEN** a package is AI-related (ralph-tui)
    62→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `ai` tag's `bun` list in `packages.yaml`
    63→
    64→#### Scenario: Development package category
    65→- **WHEN** a package is development-related
    66→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `dev` tag's `bun` list in `packages.yaml`
    67→
    68→#### Scenario: Core package category
    69→- **WHEN** a package is essential for basic operations
    70→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `core` tag's `bun` list in `packages.yaml`
    71→
    72→## MODIFIED Requirements
    73→
    74→### Requirement: Package Manager Installation Order
    75→Package managers SHALL be installed before their respective packages, following the numbered script execution sequence.
    76→
    77→#### Scenario: SDKMAN before SDKs
    78→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
    79→- **THEN** SDKMAN installation (position 20) SHALL run before SDK installation (position 24)
    80→
    81→#### Scenario: Homebrew before packages
    82→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
    83→- **THEN** Homebrew installation (pre-hook) SHALL run before package installation (position 23)
    84→
    85→#### Scenario: UV tool installation timing
    86→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
    87→- **THEN** UV tool installation (position 25) runs before UV installation (position 30)
    88→- **NOTE**: This assumes UV is available through another mechanism (e.g., Homebrew in packages.yaml)
    89→
    90→#### Scenario: Bun global package installation timing
    91→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
    92→- **THEN** Bun global package installation (position 26) SHALL run after Homebrew package installation (position 23)
    93→- **NOTE**: Bun is installed via Homebrew as a core brew (`oven-sh/bun/bun`)
    94→
    95→#### Scenario: Machine-specific Brewfile always last
    96→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
    97→- **THEN** machine-specific Brewfile installation (position 28) SHALL be the last script in the package management group (20-29)
    98→
    99→### Requirement: Complete Installation Sequence
   100→The complete package installation sequence SHALL follow this order:
   101→
   102→#### Scenario: Full installation order
   103→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   104→- **THEN** the installation sequence SHALL be:
   105→  1. Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   106→  2. Position 23: Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml
   107→  3. Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   108→  4. Position 25: Install tools via UV
   109→  5. Position 26: Install global packages via Bun
   110→  6. Position 28: Install additional machine-specific Homebrew packages
   111→  7. Position 30: Install UV itself
   112→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./openspec/specs/package-management/spec.md`

```text
     1→# Package Management System
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→The package management system provides a unified approach for installing packages across different ecosystems, all defined in a single `packages.yaml` file:
     5→- **Homebrew**: System packages, applications, and CLI tools (essential tag-based + machine-specific)
     6→- **UV Tools**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`)
     7→- **SDKMAN SDKs**: JVM ecosystem SDKs and build tools (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`, requires `dev` tag)
     8→
     9→## Requirements
    10→### Requirement: Central Package Definition
    11→All packages SHALL be defined in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` organized by platform and tag.
    12→
    13→#### Scenario: Platform-based package organization
    14→- **WHEN** packages are defined for the darwin platform
    15→- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin` with nested tag categories
    16→
    17→#### Scenario: Tag-based package categories
    18→- **WHEN** packages are categorized by tags (core, dev, ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
    19→- **THEN** each tag SHALL contain separate lists for `taps`, `brews`, `casks`, `mas`, `sdkman`, and `uv` packages
    20→
    21→### Requirement: Tag-Based Package Selection
    22→Package installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags during chezmoi initialization.
    23→
    24→#### Scenario: Core packages always installed
    25→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    26→- **THEN** packages in the `core` tag SHALL be installed regardless of other selected tags
    27→
    28→#### Scenario: Optional tag packages
    29→- **WHEN** a user selects the `dev` tag
    30→- **THEN** only packages in `core` and `dev` tags SHALL be installed
    31→- **AND** packages in unselected tags SHALL be skipped
    32→
    33→#### Scenario: Multiple tag selection
    34→- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai,personal`
    35→- **THEN** packages from all four tag categories SHALL be installed
    36→
    37→### Requirement: Homebrew Package Types
    38→The system SHALL support all Homebrew package types: taps, brews (formulae), casks, and Mac App Store apps.
    39→
    40→#### Scenario: Homebrew tap installation
    41→- **WHEN** a tap is defined in `packages.yaml` under the `taps` key
    42→- **THEN** the tap SHALL be added via `brew tap` before installing packages from that tap
    43→
    44→#### Scenario: Formula installation
    45→- **WHEN** a package is listed under `brews`
    46→- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install <package>`
    47→
    48→#### Scenario: Cask installation
    49→- **WHEN** an application is listed under `casks`
    50→- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install --cask <package>`
    51→
    52→#### Scenario: Mac App Store installation
    53→- **WHEN** an app is listed under `mas` with format `"App Name", id: 123456`
    54→- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `mas install 123456`
    55→
    56→### Requirement: Machine-Specific Brewfiles
    57→Additional packages SHALL be defined in machine-specific Brewfiles located in the `brewfiles/` directory.
    58→
    59→#### Scenario: Machine-specific Brewfile location
    60→- **WHEN** the machine configuration specifies `brewfile: mbp-brewfile`
    61→- **THEN** the system SHALL use `brewfiles/mbp-brewfile` for additional packages
    62→
    63→#### Scenario: Brewfile symlinking
    64→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    65→- **THEN** the machine-specific Brewfile SHALL be symlinked to `~/Brewfile`
    66→- **AND** SHALL be accessible for manual `brew bundle` operations
    67→
    68→### Requirement: User Confirmation for Additional Packages
    69→Machine-specific Brewfile packages SHALL require explicit user confirmation before installation.
    70→
    71→#### Scenario: Confirmation prompt
    72→- **WHEN** a machine-specific Brewfile contains additional packages
    73→- **THEN** the script SHALL prompt the user for confirmation before running `brew bundle`
    74→
    75→#### Scenario: Confirmation denied
    76→- **WHEN** the user declines the Brewfile installation prompt
    77→- **THEN** the script SHALL skip Brewfile installation
    78→- **AND** SHALL continue with other setup tasks
    79→
    80→#### Scenario: Confirmation accepted
    81→- **WHEN** the user confirms the Brewfile installation prompt
    82→- **THEN** the script SHALL execute `brew bundle --file ~/Brewfile`
    83→
    84→### Requirement: Idempotent Package Installation
    85→Package installation SHALL be idempotent and safe to re-run.
    86→
    87→#### Scenario: Already installed package
    88→- **WHEN** a package is already installed
    89→- **THEN** `brew install` SHALL skip reinstallation
    90→- **AND** SHALL display that the package is already installed
    91→
    92→#### Scenario: Package updates
    93→- **WHEN** running `brew bundle` with existing packages
    94→- **THEN** Homebrew SHALL upgrade outdated packages if specified in Brewfile options
    95→
    96→### Requirement: Homebrew Bootstrap
    97→Homebrew itself SHALL be installed via a pre-hook if not already present.
    98→
    99→#### Scenario: Homebrew missing
   100→- **WHEN** chezmoi's pre-hook runs and Homebrew is not installed
   101→- **THEN** the hook SHALL install Homebrew before proceeding
   102→
   103→#### Scenario: Homebrew exists
   104→- **WHEN** Homebrew is already installed
   105→- **THEN** the pre-hook SHALL skip installation and continue
   106→
   107→### Requirement: Package Categories
   108→Packages SHALL be organized into logical categories based on their purpose.
   109→
   110→#### Scenario: Core packages
   111→- **WHEN** packages are essential for basic system operation
   112→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `core` tag category
   113→
   114→#### Scenario: Development packages
   115→- **WHEN** packages are development tools (docker, IDEs, language tools)
   116→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `dev` tag category
   117→
   118→#### Scenario: AI/ML packages
   119→- **WHEN** packages are AI or machine learning related (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio)
   120→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `ai` tag category
   121→
   122→#### Scenario: Work packages
   123→- **WHEN** packages are enterprise or work-specific tools (Teams, Workspaces)
   124→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `work` tag category
   125→
   126→#### Scenario: Personal packages
   127→- **WHEN** packages are personal productivity tools
   128→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `personal` tag category
   129→
   130→#### Scenario: Data science packages
   131→- **WHEN** packages are data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit)
   132→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `datascience` tag category
   133→
   134→#### Scenario: Mobile/network packages
   135→- **WHEN** packages are VPN or network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard)
   136→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `mobile` tag category
   137→
   138→### Requirement: Static Package Data File
   139→The `packages.yaml` file SHALL be a static file, not a template.
   140→
   141→#### Scenario: Pre-template availability
   142→- **WHEN** chezmoi's template engine initializes
   143→- **THEN** `packages.yaml` MUST exist and be parseable
   144→- **AND** SHALL NOT require template processing
   145→
   146→### Requirement: Claude Code Native Installation
   147→The system SHALL support native installation of Claude Code (Anthropic's AI coding assistant) when the `ai` tag is selected.
   148→
   149→#### Scenario: Fresh Claude Code installation on macOS
   150→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration on macOS
   151→- **AND** the `ai` tag is selected
   152→- **AND** Claude Code is not already installed
   153→- **THEN** the installation script SHALL execute `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`
   154→- **AND** SHALL verify installation by running `claude --version`
   155→- **AND** SHALL display success message upon completion
   156→
   157→#### Scenario: Claude Code already installed
   158→- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   159→- **AND** the `claude` command is already available in PATH
   160→- **THEN** the script SHALL skip installation
   161→- **AND** SHALL display a skip message indicating Claude Code is already present
   162→- **AND** SHALL exit successfully without errors
   163→
   164→#### Scenario: Installation without ai tag
   165→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   166→- **AND** the `ai` tag is NOT selected
   167→- **THEN** the Claude Code installation script SHALL not execute
   168→- **AND** no Claude Code installation SHALL occur
   169→
   170→#### Scenario: Platform detection for installation
   171→- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   172→- **AND** the platform is macOS (darwin)
   173→- **THEN** the script SHALL use the native install command for Unix-like systems
   174→- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   175→
   176→#### Scenario: Linux/WSL installation support
   177→- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   178→- **AND** the platform is Linux or WSL
   179→- **THEN** the script SHALL use the same native install command
   180→- **AND** SHALL support Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 10+, and compatible distributions
   181→- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   182→
   183→#### Scenario: Installation failure handling
   184→- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation command fails
   185→- **THEN** the script SHALL display an error message using `print_message "error"`
   186→- **AND** SHALL exit with a non-zero status code
   187→- **AND** SHALL provide troubleshooting guidance
   188→
   189→#### Scenario: Post-installation verification
   190→- **WHEN** Claude Code installation completes successfully
   191→- **THEN** the script SHALL verify the `claude` command is available
   192→- **AND** SHALL run `claude --version` to confirm successful installation
   193→- **AND** SHALL display the installed version number
   194→
   195→### Requirement: Claude Code Script Execution Order
   196→The Claude Code installation script SHALL execute in the proper sequence within the development toolchain setup.
   197→
   198→#### Scenario: Script execution order
   199→- **WHEN** chezmoi runs installation scripts
   200→- **THEN** Claude Code installation (order 36) SHALL execute:
   201→  - **AFTER** nvm installation (order 35)
   202→  - **BEFORE** shell plugins and authentication (order 40+)
   203→- **AND** SHALL be positioned in the "Environment Managers" category (30-39)
   204→
   205→#### Scenario: Installation dependencies
   206→- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   207→- **THEN** the system SHALL have curl available (from core packages)
   208→- **AND** SHALL have bash available (system default)
   209→- **AND** SHALL NOT require Node.js or other language runtimes
   210→
   211→### Requirement: Claude Code Integration with Shared Utilities
   212→The Claude Code installation script SHALL use the shared utility functions for consistent messaging and behavior.
   213→
   214→#### Scenario: Shared utility usage
   215→- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   216→- **THEN** it SHALL source `scripts/shared-utils.sh`
   217→- **AND** SHALL use `print_message()` for all user-facing output
   218→- **AND** SHALL follow standard messaging conventions (info, success, warning, error, skip)
   219→
   220→#### Scenario: Idempotent execution
   221→- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script runs multiple times
   222→- **THEN** it SHALL safely detect existing installations
   223→- **AND** SHALL not cause errors or duplicate installations
   224→- **AND** SHALL maintain system stability
   225→
   226→### Requirement: Claude Code Configuration Management
   227→The system SHALL provide Claude Code with proper configuration and state management.
   228→
   229→#### Scenario: Installation directory structure
   230→- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed via the native method
   231→- **THEN** the binary SHALL be placed at `~/.local/bin/claude`
   232→- **AND** configuration SHALL be stored in `~/.claude-code/`
   233→- **AND** user settings SHALL be stored in `~/.claude/`
   234→
   235→#### Scenario: Auto-update behavior
   236→- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed
   237→- **THEN** auto-updates SHALL be enabled by default
   238→- **AND** updates SHALL check on startup and periodically while running
   239→- **AND** users MAY disable auto-updates via `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1` environment variable
   240→
   241→#### Scenario: PATH configuration
   242→- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed to `~/.local/bin/`
   243→- **THEN** the directory SHALL be included in the user's PATH
   244→- **AND** the `claude` command SHALL be immediately accessible
   245→- **AND** SHALL work without requiring shell restart
   246→
   247→## UV Tool Management
   248→
   249→### Requirement: UV Installation
   250→The `uv` package manager SHALL be installed before UV tools are installed.
   251→
   252→#### Scenario: UV not present
   253→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration and `uv` is not installed
   254→- **THEN** the system SHALL install `uv` via the official Astral.sh installer
   255→- **AND** SHALL run at position 30 in the script execution order
   256→
   257→#### Scenario: UV already installed
   258→- **WHEN** `uv` is already present on the system
   259→- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   260→
   261→### Requirement: UV Tool Definition
   262→UV tools SHALL be defined within tag categories in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `uv` key.
   263→
   264→#### Scenario: Tool organization structure
   265→- **WHEN** tools are defined in `packages.yaml`
   266→- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.<tag>.uv` with lists of tool specifications
   267→
   268→#### Scenario: Tool specification formats
   269→- **WHEN** a tool is defined
   270→- **THEN** it SHALL use one of these formats:
   271→  - Simple package name: `"package-name"`
   272→  - Git repository: `"git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"`
   273→  - Version-pinned: `"package-name==1.0.0"`
   274→
   275→### Requirement: Tag-Based UV Tool Selection
   276→UV tool installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags.
   277→
   278→#### Scenario: Tag-conditional tools
   279→- **WHEN** a user selects the `ai` tag
   280→- **THEN** tools in the `ai` tag's `uv` list SHALL be installed
   281→- **AND** tools in unselected tag categories SHALL be skipped
   282→
   283→#### Scenario: Multiple tool categories
   284→- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai`
   285→- **THEN** UV tools from all three tag categories SHALL be installed via `uv tool install`
   286→
   287→### Requirement: UV Tool Installation Script
   288→UV tools SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 25.
   289→
   290→#### Scenario: Tool installation execution
   291→- **WHEN** the tool installation script runs
   292→- **THEN** it SHALL execute `uv tool install <tool>` for each selected tool
   293→- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   294→
   295→#### Scenario: Tool installation idempotency
   296→- **WHEN** a tool is already installed
   297→- **THEN** `uv tool install` SHALL skip reinstallation or upgrade the tool
   298→- **AND** SHALL continue with remaining tools
   299→
   300→### Requirement: UV Tool Categories
   301→UV tools SHALL be organized within the same tag categories as Homebrew packages in `packages.yaml`.
   302→
   303→#### Scenario: AI tool category
   304→- **WHEN** a tool is AI-related (claude-monitor, zsh-llm-suggestions)
   305→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `ai` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   306→
   307→#### Scenario: Development tool category
   308→- **WHEN** a tool is development-related (linters, formatters, build tools)
   309→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `dev` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   310→
   311→#### Scenario: Core tool category
   312→- **WHEN** a tool is essential for basic operations
   313→- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `core` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   314→
   315→## SDKMAN SDK Management
   316→
   317→### Requirement: SDKMAN Installation
   318→SDKMAN SHALL be installed before SDKs are installed, and only on machines with the `dev` tag.
   319→
   320→#### Scenario: SDKMAN installation with dev tag
   321→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration with the `dev` tag selected
   322→- **THEN** the system SHALL install SDKMAN via the official installer
   323→- **AND** SHALL run at position 20 in the script execution order
   324→
   325→#### Scenario: SDKMAN skipped without dev tag
   326→- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   327→- **THEN** SDKMAN installation SHALL be skipped entirely
   328→
   329→#### Scenario: SDKMAN already installed
   330→- **WHEN** SDKMAN is already present on the system
   331→- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   332→
   333→### Requirement: SDK Definition
   334→SDKs SHALL be defined within the `dev` tag in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `sdkman` key.
   335→
   336→#### Scenario: SDK organization structure
   337→- **WHEN** SDKs are defined in `packages.yaml`
   338→- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` with a list of SDK specifications
   339→
   340→#### Scenario: SDK specification format
   341→- **WHEN** an SDK is defined
   342→- **THEN** it SHALL use the format: `"sdk-name version-identifier"`
   343→- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"`, `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`, `"liquibase"`
   344→
   345→### Requirement: Tag-Based SDK Selection
   346→SDK installation SHALL require the `dev` tag.
   347→
   348→#### Scenario: Dev tag required
   349→- **WHEN** SDKs are being installed
   350→- **THEN** the `dev` tag MUST be selected
   351→- **AND** the installation script SHALL skip execution if `dev` tag is not present
   352→
   353→#### Scenario: Development SDK category
   354→- **WHEN** SDKs are listed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   355→- **THEN** they SHALL be installed when the `dev` tag is selected
   356→
   357→### Requirement: SDK Installation Script
   358→SDKs SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 24.
   359→
   360→#### Scenario: SDK installation execution
   361→- **WHEN** the SDK installation script runs with `dev` tag
   362→- **THEN** it SHALL execute `sdk install <sdk> <version>` for each defined SDK
   363→- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   364→
   365→#### Scenario: SDK installation idempotency
   366→- **WHEN** an SDK version is already installed
   367→- **THEN** `sdk install` SHALL skip reinstallation
   368→- **AND** SHALL display that the SDK is already installed
   369→
   370→### Requirement: SDKMAN Shell Integration
   371→SDKMAN SHALL be initialized in shell profiles to make SDKs available in interactive sessions.
   372→
   373→#### Scenario: Shell profile initialization
   374→- **WHEN** a shell profile (.bash_profile, .zshrc) is loaded
   375→- **THEN** it SHALL source `~/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh` if it exists
   376→- **AND** SHALL set `SDKMAN_DIR` environment variable
   377→
   378→#### Scenario: Conditional shell integration
   379→- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   380→- **THEN** SDKMAN initialization SHALL NOT be included in shell profiles
   381→
   382→### Requirement: SDK Categories
   383→SDKs SHALL be defined under the `dev` tag's `sdkman` key in `packages.yaml`.
   384→
   385→#### Scenario: Java SDK versions
   386→- **WHEN** multiple Java versions are needed
   387→- **THEN** they SHALL be defined separately under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   388→- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"` and `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`
   389→
   390→#### Scenario: Build tools
   391→- **WHEN** JVM build tools are needed (Liquibase, Gradle, Maven)
   392→- **THEN** they SHALL be placed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` in `packages.yaml`
   393→
   394→## Script Execution Order
   395→
   396→### Requirement: Package Manager Installation Order
   397→Package managers SHALL be installed before their respective packages, following the numbered script execution sequence.
   398→
   399→#### Scenario: SDKMAN before SDKs
   400→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   401→- **THEN** SDKMAN installation (position 20) SHALL run before SDK installation (position 24)
   402→
   403→#### Scenario: Homebrew before packages
   404→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   405→- **THEN** Homebrew installation (pre-hook) SHALL run before package installation (position 23)
   406→
   407→#### Scenario: UV tool installation timing
   408→- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   409→- **THEN** UV tool installation (position 25) runs before UV installation (position 30)
   410→- **NOTE**: This assumes UV is available through another mechanism (e.g., Homebrew in packages.yaml)
   411→
   412→### Requirement: Complete Installation Sequence
   413→The complete package installation sequence SHALL follow this order:
   414→
   415→#### Scenario: Full installation order
   416→- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   417→- **THEN** the installation sequence SHALL be:
   418→  1. Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   419→  2. Position 23: Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml
   420→  3. Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   421→  4. Position 25: Install tools via UV
   422→  5. Position 26: Install additional machine-specific Homebrew packages
   423→  6. Position 30: Install UV itself
   424→
   425→## Design Decisions
   426→
   427→### Three-Layer Package Management Rationale
   428→Using three complementary package management systems provides ecosystem-specific optimization:
   429→
   430→#### Homebrew (System Layer)
   431→- **Purpose**: System packages, GUI applications, fonts, CLI utilities
   432→- **Strengths**: Native macOS integration, wide package availability, cask support for applications
   433→- **Dual approach**: Essential tag-based packages + machine-specific Brewfiles
   434→- **Flexibility**: Users can add machine-specific packages without modifying the main repository
   435→
   436→#### UV Tools (Python Ecosystem)
   437→- **Purpose**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities
   438→- **Strengths**: Fast, modern Python package manager; isolated tool installations
   439→- **Pattern**: Tag-based selection with `core` always installed
   440→- **Use cases**: AI tools (claude-monitor), shell utilities (zsh-llm-suggestions), Python dev tools
   441→
   442→#### SDKMAN (JVM Ecosystem)
   443→- **Purpose**: Java SDKs, build tools, and JVM-related packages
   444→- **Strengths**: Version management for multiple Java installations, JVM ecosystem focus
   445→- **Pattern**: Requires `dev` tag, platform-specific definitions
   446→- **Use cases**: Multiple Java versions, Gradle, Maven, Liquibase, other JVM tools
   447→
   448→### Ecosystem Separation Benefits
   449→Separating package management by ecosystem provides:
   450→- **Optimized tools**: Each package manager specializes in its ecosystem
   451→- **Version isolation**: Multiple Java versions (SDKMAN), isolated Python tools (UV)
   452→- **Reduced conflicts**: System packages don't interfere with language-specific tools
   453→- **Clear responsibility**: Each layer has a well-defined purpose
   454→
   455→### Tag-Based Installation
   456→Using tags for package selection enables:
   457→- Customized installations for different machine purposes (work vs. personal vs. development)
   458→- Easy addition of new package categories
   459→- Minimal setup reduces installation time and disk usage
   460→- Clear categorization of package purposes
   461→
   462→### Machine-Specific Brewfiles
   463→Separate Brewfiles per machine allow:
   464→- Machine-specific tools without cluttering the main package list
   465→- User confirmation before installing potentially large or unwanted packages
   466→- Easy experimentation with new tools on one machine
   467→- Brewfile symlink enables manual `brew bundle` commands
   468→
   469→### Static Data File
   470→Making the package data file (`packages.yaml`) static ensures:
   471→- Available before template engine runs
   472→- Simple YAML editing without template syntax
   473→- Clear separation between data and logic
   474→- Easier for tools to parse and validate
   475→- Single source of truth for all package definitions
   476→
   477→### Script Execution Order Design
   478→The numbered script execution order (20, 23, 24, 25, 26, 30) uses 10-point range grouping:
   479→- **Categorical grouping**: Scripts in 20-29 range handle package management, 30-39 handle environment managers
   480→- **Flexible spacing**: Within each range, scripts use 1-5 point spacing as needed (20, 23, 24, 25, 26)
   481→- **Future expansion**: Gaps within ranges allow inserting new scripts without renumbering
   482→- **Dependency management**: Package managers install before their packages
   483→- **Predictable order**: Clear, documented sequence for troubleshooting
   484→
   485→**Note on UV timing**: Position 25 (UV tools) runs before position 30 (UV installation). This implies UV must be available through another mechanism (e.g., installed via Homebrew in packages.yaml).
   486→
   487→## Tag Reference
   488→
   489→### Tag Usage Across Package Managers
   490→
   491→This table shows which tags control package installation across all three package management systems:
   492→
   493→| Tag | Homebrew | UV Tools | SDKMAN | Description |
   494→|-----|----------|----------|--------|-------------|
   495→| `core` | ✅ Always | ✅ Always | ❌ | Essential packages for basic system operation |
   496→| `dev` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ✅ **Required** | Development tools, IDEs, language toolchains |
   497→| `ai` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ❌ | AI/ML tools (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio) |
   498→| `work` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ❌ | Enterprise/work tools (Teams, Workspaces) |
   499→| `personal` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ❌ | Personal productivity |
   500→| `datascience` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ❌ | Data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit) |
   501→| `mobile` | ✅ Optional | ✅ Optional | ❌ | VPN and network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard) |
   502→
   503→### Tag Selection Behavior
   504→
   505→#### Core Tag (Always Active)
   506→- **Homebrew**: Core packages always installed
   507→- **UV Tools**: Core tools always installed
   508→- **SDKMAN**: Not applicable (requires `dev` tag)
   509→
   510→#### Development Tag (dev)
   511→- **Homebrew**: Installs development packages (Docker, IDEs, etc.)
   512→- **UV Tools**: Installs development tools
   513→- **SDKMAN**: **Required** - SDKMAN itself and all SDKs only install with `dev` tag
   514→
   515→#### Other Tags (ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   516→- **Homebrew**: Each tag installs corresponding category packages
   517→- **UV Tools**: Each tag installs corresponding category tools
   518→- **SDKMAN**: Not used (only `dev` category exists)
   519→
   520→### Data File Structure
   521→
   522→#### packages.yaml (Unified Package Definition)
   523→```yaml
   524→packages:
   525→  darwin:
   526→    taps:
   527→    - 'isen-ng/dotnet-sdk-versions'
   528→    - 'buo/cask-upgrade'
   529→
   530→    core:
   531→      taps: [...]
   532→      brews: [...]
   533→      casks: [...]
   534→      mas: [...]
   535→
   536→    dev:
   537→      taps: [...]
   538→      brews: [...]
   539→      casks: [...]
   540→      mas: [...]
   541→      sdkman:       # SDKMAN SDKs (only in dev tag)
   542→      - "java 25-tem"
   543→      - "java 8.0.462-zulu"
   544→      - "liquibase"
   545→
   546→    ai:
   547→      taps: [...]
   548→      brews: [...]
   549→      casks: [...]
   550→      uv:           # UV Python tools (can be in any tag)
   551→      - "basic-memory"
   552→      - "claude-monitor"
   553→      - "git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"
   554→
   555→    # ... other tags (work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   556→```
   557→
   558→### Installation Scripts
   559→
   560→| Script | Position | Frequency | Purpose |
   561→|--------|----------|-----------|---------|
   562→| `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl` | 20 | Once | Install SDKMAN (requires `dev` tag) |
   563→| `run_onchange_before_darwin-23-install-packages.sh.tmpl` | 23 | On change | Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml |
   564→| `run_onchange_before_darwin-24-install-sdks.sh.tmpl` | 24 | On change | Install SDKs via SDKMAN from packages.yaml (requires `dev` tag) |
   565→| `run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl` | 25 | On change | Install UV tools from packages.yaml |
   566→| `run_onchange_before_darwin-26-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl` | 26 | On change | Install machine-specific Homebrew packages |
   567→| `run_once_before_darwin-30-install-uv.sh.tmpl` | 30 | Once | Install UV package manager |
   568→
```
</details></tool-use>
